name: Belt Nyah (Descending)
pedagogy: A bright, twangy belt-and-twang drill on a descending 5-4-3-2-1 scale. The nasalized 'nyah' (/njæ/) narrows the aryepiglottic funnel, boosting the 2–4 kHz 'ring' that makes belt carry — an 'Edge'-style brighter, sharper set-up. Descending from the top keeps it released. Source: Broadway belt pedagogy (Roll 2016, Journal of Voice) + Estill twang. The app scores pitch, not the twang brightness itself.
